schema: Per-round season store. Each round is its own file in this folder (canonical record for that LOCATION). This index lists round order + metadata + which rounds carry a sprint. Engine fetches the index, then loads round files. Ricky updates exactly ONE round file per weekend. Kept per-file well under the 30KB agent-readback cap so the store scales to 22 rounds + future times/telemetry without a monolith.
standings: COMPUTED live (never stored): WDC = sum of race+sprint points per driverId across round files; WCC = sum per team.
provenance_verified: Every finishing position + points re-verified vs formula1.com, FIA classification/championship-points PDFs, and RaceFans full-order tables (2026-07-07). Sprint points reconcile every driver's official season total to the exact figure. THREE store errors corrected vs the old f1-racetracks/data.json: Austria (Russell won, not Antonelli), Spain/Catalunya (Hamilton maiden Ferrari win; Antonelli DNF from P2), Monaco (P3 Gasly, penalties overturned on appeal; store had Hadjar).

open_flags.UNRESOLVED-DUP: Rows for Colapinto, Lindblad, Bottas, Perez carry driverId:null + _anchor:'UNRESOLVED-DUP'. Duplicate driver tasks exist in the F1 Drivers list (two Colapinto, two Bottas, two Lindblad; Perez single but in the newer ID block), so a 1:1 resolve isn't possible yet and IDs were NOT guessed (anchor convention: unresolved = STOP-and-flag). Dedup the list, confirm canonical IDs, then backfill. Likely-canonical originals in the established 86ae52* block: Colapinto 86ae52rq5, Bottas 86ae52rtd, Lindblad 86ae52ryh; Perez only 86aj1ra1z exists.
